meals.label: Meal A
meals.name: Easy Weeknight Salmon Sushi Bowls
ings: Sriracha mayo, toasted sesame seeds, 1 nori sheet, pickled ginger
steps: Cook rice in rice cooker. Combine vinegar, sugar, ½ tsp salt; microwave 20 sec, stir until dissolved. Pour over rice. Let sit.
Panko crunchies: heat oil in small skillet, toast panko ~2 min until golden.
Build bowls: ¾ cup rice, flaked salmon, ¼ cup each cucumbers and carrots, ½ avocado, ginger, panko. Top with nori and sesame seeds. Drizzle sriracha mayo.
Sriracha mayo: mix ½ cup mayo, 1 Tbsp sriracha, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp low-sodium soy sauce.
meals.note: 💡 Serve sriracha mayo on the side for adults only — kids can have it plain with extra soy on the rice. Pre-cooked salmon makes this a true 20-minute meal.

meals.label: Meal B
meals.name: Air Fryer Fish Tacos with Cilantro Lime Slaw
ings: Extra salt and pepper to taste
steps: Spray air fryer basket with oil. Mix all spices in a small bowl.
Pat fish dry, lay in basket. Brush with olive oil, coat with spice rub, press gently to adhere. Spritz with oil.
Preheat air fryer to 400°F. Cook fish 8–10 min until opaque and flakes at 145°F. Do not flip.
While fish cooks, toss together all slaw ingredients. Adjust lime and salt to taste.
Cut fish into pieces. Serve in tortillas topped with slaw and extra lime.
meals.note: 💡 Cayenne omitted from this version to keep it kid-friendly — still flavorful from chili powder, paprika, and cumin. No air fryer? Bake at 400°F for ~15 min.

meals.label: Meal C
meals.name: Thai Peanut Chicken Wraps
ings: 1 lb boneless chicken breast, cut into strips
steps: Whisk peanut butter, 2 Tbsp soy sauce, 1 Tbsp sesame oil, lime juice, garlic, ginger, and honey in a medium bowl until smooth. Set aside.
Cut chicken into thin strips and toss with remaining 1 Tbsp soy sauce and 1 Tbsp sesame oil. Marinate 10 min while you prep vegetables.
Wash and dry lettuce, finely shred cabbage, julienne carrots, chop cilantro. Set out in separate bowls for easy assembly.
Heat large skillet over medium-high. Cook chicken strips 6–8 min, stirring occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through. Let cool slightly.
Warm tortillas 30 sec per side in dry skillet, or wrap in damp paper towel and microwave 20 sec until pliable.
Spread 2 Tbsp peanut sauce down center of each tortilla. Layer with lettuce, cabbage, carrots, chicken, and cilantro. Drizzle with more sauce.
Fold in sides and roll tightly from the bottom. Cut in half diagonally. Serve with extra peanut sauce on the side for dipping.
meals.note: 💡 Peanut sauce keeps a week in the fridge — make a double batch. Pat veggies dry so wraps don't get soggy. For kids: assemble with less sauce or serve as a deconstructed bowl over rice if they're wary of wraps.
